title: Supply Chain, Browser Binaries, Packages, Containers, and Rollout · Inference Brief: Package Inspection

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect packed files, exports, declarations, source maps, licenses, fixtures, secrets, generated files, and emitted smoke before publish.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Package Inspection model · Theory Package Inspec…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Package Inspection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Package Inspection model · Theory Package Inspec…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5758 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove dependency and browser provenance, package contents, least-authority containers, and immutable rollout; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect packed files, exports, declarations, source maps, licenses, fixtures, secrets, generated files, and emitted smoke before publish.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Package Inspection model · Theory Package Inspec…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A successful npm pack means package contents are correct.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-ts-package-inspection-ub3r35.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-package-inspection-ub3r35(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_package_inspection_ub3r35\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?artifactGates < 15)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?passedGates < 15 \|\| passedGates > artifactGates)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?platformCases < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?untrackedArtifacts > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
